text:
NANTERRE, France 芒聙聰 After three straight Olympic gold medals, perhaps the most stunning run of dominance by a dynasty in sports, theU.S. women芒聙聶s water polo teamwill do something that might be even harder: try to win bronze.脗

Thursday芒聙聶s 14芒聙聯13 semifinal loss to Australia in penalty shots left the Americans grappling with a challenge this group has rarely faced. They will have to regroup after a defeat.脗

芒聙聹I always think a bronze medal is super difficult, because you don't come for a bronze medal,芒聙聺 said captain and attacker Maggie Steffens. 芒聙聹You come for the gold.芒聙聺

For most of her career, she has achieved it. Steffens, 31, is the only player who remains from the London, Rio and Tokyo squads. This team is younger and less experienced than the previous three and, as coach Adam Krikorian has admitted, not as talented as the earlier juggernauts. It has tried to emphasize that it is not a continuation of what came before. It wanted to appreciate the program芒聙聶s legacy without letting expectations burden it. The players are a new group with new goals. They were not caught up in trying to extend a streak. 芒聙聹This team was just trying to win their first one,芒聙聺 said Krikorian.脗

They are also managing challenges much more serious than any they will face in the pool. Steffens is mourning the death of her husband芒聙聶s sister, Lulu Conner, who died of what Steffens has called a 芒聙聹medical emergency芒聙聺 after traveling to Paris for the Olympics. Attacker Maddie Musselman is supporting her husband, former UCLA water polo player Patrick Woepse, as he undergoes treatment for Stage IV lung cancer.脗

芒聙聹Unfortunately, there's been a lot of adversity outside of water polo that we芒聙聶ve faced,芒聙聺 Steffens said. 芒聙聹We芒聙聶ve been through a lot and done a great job of being there for one another and now we get another chance to do that.芒聙聺

Not everyone was entirely clear as to how to go about that.脗

芒聙聹I芒聙聶ll let you know when I know,芒聙聺 said defender Jordan Raney.

Once the shock wears off芒聙聰Steffens said they will give themselves the rest of the night to stew芒聙聰they will begin analyzing what went wrong Thursday. Aside from a pool-play loss to Spain, this team had largely found clutch performances when it needed them. In the semifinals, though, the Americans led 6芒聙聯2 at halftime but failed to shut the door defensively. And the attack, often so devastating, played tight, a condition Krikorian attributed to nerves.脗

芒聙聹I feel like our offense was a little bit stagnant,芒聙聺 said attacker Jewel Roemer. 芒聙聹And we didn芒聙聶t really convert on a lot of our six-on-five opportunities, which we take advantage of most other games.芒聙聺

Australia tied the game at eight with 2:54 left in the fourth period, and goalie Ashleigh Johnson managed to keep it there as the clock expired. The Aussies and the Americans traded successful penalty shots until, on the U.S.芒聙聶s sixth attempt, Australia goalie Gabriella Palm got a hand on Musselman芒聙聶s shot. The Australians went nuts. The Americans looked unsure what to do.脗

芒聙聹This is the first time we芒聙聶ve had to feel this feeling in some time,芒聙聺 said Krikorian.脗聽 He added, 芒聙聹This is the reality of life. You don't sit on top of the podium every single time. We芒聙聶ve made it look easy at times, but it芒聙聶s never been easy.芒聙聺

This Olympic push has been among the hardest. Afterthe exhaustion of a COVID-19 Olympics in Tokyo, many of the veterans considered retiring; legendary centers Aria Fischer and Melissa Seidemann did, leaving the team without much experience up the middle. A condensed, three-year cycle combined with a young team has meant accelerating a lot of learning.脗

Now they will try to learn how to rebound from a loss and end the Olympics on Sunday with a win芒聙聰and bronze芒聙聰against the Netherlands.

芒聙聹We芒聙聶ve always talked about wanting to have the finished product on August 10,芒聙聺 Steffens said. 芒聙聹Unfortunately, you want that in the gold medal game. But now we get another opportunity to try to show the world who we are.芒聙聺

Stephanie Apstein is a senior writer covering baseball and Olympic sports for Sports Illustrated, where she started as an intern in 2011 and has since covered a dozen World Series and three Olympics. She has twice won top honors from the Associated Press Sports Editors, and her work has been included in the Best American Sports Writing book series. She graduated from Trinity College with a bachelor芒聙聶s in French and Italian, and has a master芒聙聶s in journalism from Columbia University.

title: U.S. Women芒聙聶s Water Polo芒聙聶s Decade-Long Dominance Comes to an End in Paris
description: After its hopes of an Olympic four-peat were dashed by Australia, Team USA now finds itself playing for an unfamiliar title: a bronze medal.
